publications.editedComment: The room agrees on a narrow division of labor. Four preset buttons ask the Server store to prepare COMFY, A1111, OPENAI, or ANTHROPIC records, open the shared form, and reveal `add-server`. Two computed summaries report the currently active Art and Text servers by label or title plus type, falling back to explicit no-selection messages. The full `server-gallery` remains below for browsing and management. On mount, remote server state loads only when the store has not already initialized. This Component does not decide capabilities or billing; its own heading says routes and mana own those concerns. Consensus reached: it is a launcher and status overview around the existing server tools.

BAM—four preset hammers, one shared forge. Comfy, A1111, OpenAI, and Anthropic buttons call `createNewServer(type)`, open the Server-store form, and mount `add-server`. The two status plates read `activeArtServer` and `activeTextServer`, showing label or title with server type or a clear empty state. The gallery beneath handles the broader collection. Mount initializes remote server data only when it has not already loaded. This surface does not test endpoints, route generations, or calculate mana. It gets a record onto the anvil and tells you which Art and Text tools are currently equipped.

Corvid's inventory sheet accepts one required name and a broad set of optional identifiers: custom label, local path, Civitai, Hugging Face, custom and media URLs, description, generation notes, maturity, resource type, and supported server family. Empty optional strings become null before the payload is sent. The current user id is attached when available. Submission is blocked while saving or without a trimmed name, then delegates to `checkpointStore.createCheckpointResource`. Success announces the save and emits the created resource; failure exposes the thrown message. The optional close button only emits `close`. No file is uploaded and no model is loaded here—the form records where a resource is and what it is for.

Entry requirements are explicit. `name` is the only mandatory text field; every other optional string is trimmed and posted as null when empty. The record classifies the resource as checkpoint, LoRA, LyCORIS, embedding, hypernetwork, sampler, ControlNet, URL, or API, and separately records its supported server family. URLs, paths, preview media, notes, maturity, and current user id are included in one payload. The Checkpoint store performs creation. A successful return is emitted through `saved`; errors become the form note; `isSaving` is always released. This ledger registers metadata and location. It neither downloads the asset nor validates that an external URL actually resolves.

A whole color toybox! The form edits the Theme store's shared `themeForm`: name, room, tagline, light/dark scheme, public flag, and every configured color key. Color inputs update the store immediately, while the live preview converts hex values into DaisyUI-style CSS variables without saving. Randomize fills the form; Reset restores it. Save is disabled without a trimmed name and can update an existing id or create a new theme, with the store's apply-after-save preference available as a toggle. A separate Preview button applies the current unsaved palette. Errors remain visible in the form. This Component builds, previews, and delegates persistence—it does not hide a second theme model behind the glitter.

Exhibit controls are grouped into metadata, palette, preview, and persistence. The Component initializes the Theme store form, renders every store-defined color key, and writes color changes through `setColorValue`. The preview derives background, text, and DaisyUI variable values from the unsaved form. Randomize and Reset operate through store helpers. Save requires a nonblank name and selects create or update based on `themeForm.id`; the apply-after-save toggle is also store-backed. Preview applies the working form separately from persistence. The error panel reports failed operations. Visitors should note that the preview is an active theme application, not merely a static card, while saving remains an explicit second action.

reviewer.name: The Navigator of the Last Bus

publications.editedComment: Two stops only, friend: gallery and custom. The active stop comes from the Nav store's `theme` dashboard tab and falls back to gallery when invalid. Gallery edit events move to custom. The custom stop hosts `add-theme`, shows whether the form is creating or editing, and lets a shared Theme be selected into the store form. Serialized theme values are parsed defensively; malformed or non-object values become an empty palette. New Theme resets the form. `forgeKey` forces the editor to remount after loading or resetting. Mount initializes navigation and refreshes Theme-store data in parallel, with loading and error states visible. This bus coordinates the route and cargo; the gallery and forge do the actual theme work.

Ah, a two-room establishment with respectable paperwork. The Nav store chooses gallery or custom, and any dubious tab is escorted back to gallery. Theme counts combine built-in and shared records. Refresh calls `themeStore.getThemes` under an explicit loading/error wrapper. In the custom room, selecting a shared Theme copies its metadata and safely parsed values into `themeForm`, then increments a key to remount the forge. New Theme resets the form and does the same. The gallery's edit event changes rooms; the manager itself does not apply or save palettes. It is maître d', inventory counter, and form loader—never the chef pretending to be all three.

The portfolio is routed by one validated Giftshop dashboard tab. Community mounts About and Sponsor pages; Giftshop mounts the storefront; Mana mounts wallet, top-up, and subscription surfaces; Forum is an explicit placeholder. On mount, Cart, User, and Nav stores initialize together. Mana-topup and subscription query results switch to the Mana tab, display success or cancellation notices, remove the consumed query parameter, and refresh user data only after subscription success. Loading and errors expose a retry path. The summary reports current cart items and formatted total. This manager does not sell a product or confirm a payment itself; it allocates the dashboard and consumes trusted return signals from the checkout routes.
